intro: Crepidium warapussae, also known as Warapussa orchid, is a terrestrial orchid species endemic to the tropical forests of Thailand. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Crepidium warapussae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
